anchor_snippets: Newly graduated AI/software engineer specializing in Python, RAG pipelines, and LLM integration. I have built an AI-powered GitLab repository analyzer at Plejd AB, combining backend engineering with applied machine learning. Comfortable delivering production-facing tools and presenting technical progress clearly.

I have experience in technical work from my role as an IT administrator at CNSS Solutions, where I handled system maintenance and provided technical support. As a forklift driver at Dagab AB, I have developed accuracy and the ability to work independently under pressure. Additionally, I spend time programming in my spare time, building various programs. I have a manual class B driver's license and speak fluent Swedish and English.

What about easier games to make, like 2D games? Another thought I had is: instead of using a full LLM model that might need a lot of prompt engineering and fine-tuning, what if instead we use a couple of highly tuned and optimized ML models instead? Maybe it is already used in the current game market and nothing new, but for an indie game and home PC computers, this might be a better idea.

I have never made a video game before. But I have an idea, and it is a big idea. My thought is to make a horror game with an AI ghost or AI companion that you can speak to through text or speech. Based on instructions and rules given to the AI, if you say the right thing or ask the right question, you might get clues for how to beat the next boss or solve the next puzzle. It is still a fresh idea, but do you believe this is something viable?
